content.cn_text: 在我们“科学史”课程的最后一讲中，我想展望一下
content.en_text: In this final lecture of our course on the History of Science, I would like to look

content.cn_text: 未来，并向大家介绍一些关于科学发展的预测。
content.en_text: to the future and tell you about some predictions that have been made about scientific developments.

content.cn_text: 我这次讲座的内容基于一项研究，在这项研究中，30位科学领袖受邀
content.en_text: The content for my talk is based on some research in which 30 scientific leaders were invited

content.cn_text: 评论他们认为未来可能会在自己学科中出现的发展，
content.en_text: to comment on the developments that they envisage as likely to come about in their own discipline

content.cn_text: 时间范围是未来50年内。在为大家总结其中一些最有趣的观点之后，
content.en_text: within 50 years. After summarizing some of their most interesting contributions for you,

content.cn_text: 我会请大家发表看法，谈谈这些变化在
content.en_text: I'll ask you to give me your opinions as to the likelihood of these changes occurring within

content.cn_text: 这个时间范围内发生的可能性。你们也可以自己提出其他可能的变化。
content.en_text: this time scale. You may also like to suggest some other likely changes yourselves.

content.cn_text: 首先，我们从计算机科学开始，因为这显然是一个必然会出现
content.en_text: So first, let's start with computer science as this is clearly an area where there are bound

content.cn_text: 重大发展的领域，而且更重要的是，这些发展可能会影响
content.en_text: to be dramatic developments, and even more significantly perhaps, these developments are likely to impact

content.cn_text: 所有其他学习领域。计算机的发明代表着人类事业中的一次巨大飞跃，
content.en_text: on every other field of learning. The invention of computers represents an enormous leap forward

content.cn_text: 它确实可以被认为是一项极其重要的发展，
content.en_text: in the field of human endeavor. It could indeed be considered as being as momentous a development

content.cn_text: 就像我们的洞穴祖先最初学会生火并把火用于各种用途一样重要。
content.en_text: as when our predecessors in caves first learned to make fire and use it for all sorts of purposes.

content.cn_text: 未来，计算革命必将被普遍认为是一场变革，
content.en_text: In the future, the computational revolution is certain to be generally recognized as a transformation

content.cn_text: 其重要性可与工业革命相提并论。我提到的研究中的计算机科学家
content.en_text: as significant as, say, the industrial revolution. The computer scientists in the study I referred to

content.cn_text: 认为在未来几十年中，自动推理系统
content.en_text: felt that in the coming decades, lives will be significantly enhanced by automated reasoning systems

content.cn_text: 会显著改善人们的生活。大家相当一致地认为，人们会越来越多地把
content.en_text: of different types. There was considerable agreement that people will increasingly perceive

content.cn_text: 这些计算机系统看作是智能的。
content.en_text: these computer systems as intelligent.

content.cn_text: 虽然其中许多系统会在幕后运行，但另一些会出现在前台，以优雅且常常协作的方式帮助人们
content.en_text: Although many of these will be deployed behind the scenes, others will be in the foreground, serving in an elegant, often collaborative manner to help people

content.cn_text: 完成工作、学习和教学、反思和记忆、规划和决策。
content.en_text: do their jobs, to learn and teach, to reflect and remember, to plan and decide.

content.cn_text: 这里暂时不进一步展开细节，我们转向心理学家的预测。
content.en_text: Without going into further detail about this at this point, let's turn to what predictions psychologists had to make.

content.cn_text: 他们的关键观点是，心理学研究将不可避免地变得越来越多样化。
content.en_text: Their key point was that psychological research would inevitably become increasingly varied.

content.cn_text: 它将进入行为经济学和社会神经科学等领域。
content.en_text: It would move into such areas as behavioral economics and social neuroscience.

content.cn_text: 它还可能进一步扩展到更具实际意义的领域，
content.en_text: There is likely to be further expansion into domains with more practical relevance,

content.cn_text: 因为越来越多的女性会成为研究科学家。
content.en_text: as increasing numbers of women become research scientists.

content.cn_text: 因此，健康、环境、家庭和社会身份等领域将获得更多关注。
content.en_text: So areas such as health, environment, family and social identity will receive more attention.

content.cn_text: 在拓宽视野的同时，心理学家也会加强探索，
content.en_text: At the same time as they broaden their horizons, psychologists will also intensify their search to

content.cn_text: 以发现究竟是什么决定了我们的行为。他们将在这方面得到成像设备的帮助，
content.en_text: discover what exactly determines how we act. They will be assisted in this by imaging devices

content.cn_text: 这些设备会被植入大脑，并提供大量关于大脑如何运作的信息。
content.en_text: that will be inserted into the brain and provide a great deal of information about how it behaves.

content.cn_text: 很快应该会有一些引人入胜的发现出现。
content.en_text: There should be some fascinating discoveries coming to light very soon.

content.cn_text: 另一个目前正在经历巨大变化的领域是遗传学。
content.en_text: Another field that is currently undergoing enormous changes is that of genetics.

content.cn_text: 平均预期寿命可能会在大多数国家继续上升，这并不是新观点。
content.en_text: It is not new to suggest that average life expectancy is likely to continue rising in most countries.

content.cn_text: 然而，如今遗传学家强调，随着社会中
content.en_text: However, today geneticists emphasize that it is vital that the increasing number of the

content.cn_text: 老年人数量增加，必须考虑生活质量。那么遗传学将如何帮助实现这一点呢？
content.en_text: elderly in society must take quality of life into account. So how will genetics help this to happen?

content.cn_text: 首先，他们希望开发能够极低成本测序每个人基因组的技术，
content.en_text: To start with, they hope to develop technologies that can sequence each person's genome extremely

content.cn_text: 这些关于我们 DNA 的信息使医疗服务提供者能够预测我们
content.en_text: cheaply. This information about our DNA enables healthcare providers to predict our likelihood of

content.cn_text: 患上某种特定疾病的可能性。
content.en_text: suffering from any particular disease.

content.cn_text: 从那以后，就需要权衡各种因素，以确定可以采取什么措施来降低每个人的风险水平。
content.en_text: From there on, it is a matter of weighing up various factors with a view to determining what can be done to minimize each individual's level of risk.

content.cn_text: 掌握这些信息后，新的治疗方法将被开发出来，而目前这种一刀切的
content.en_text: With such information in hand, new treatments will be developed, and the current one-size-fits-all

content.cn_text: 医疗方法将被更加个人化的方法取代。
content.en_text: approach to healthcare will give way to a more personal approach.

content.cn_text: 我想提醒大家注意的最后一个学科领域是动物学。
content.en_text: The final subject area that I wish to bring to your attention is that of zoology.

content.cn_text: 再次，计算机和技术在这里发挥着关键作用。专家特别提到了卫星对于该领域未来
content.en_text: Once again, computers and technology have a key role to play here. Experts comment specifically on the importance of satellites for future

content.cn_text: 发展的重要性。这些技术使人们能够从家中、办公室
content.en_text: developments in this field. These enable people to explore the world from their homes, offices,

content.cn_text: 和学校探索世界。例如，现在可以上网亲眼看到
content.en_text: and schools. It is now possible, for instance, to go online and see for yourself the abundant

content.cn_text: 东非某一地区丰富的野生动物，以及周边地区人类造成的破坏，
content.en_text: wildlife in one part of East Africa, and in the surrounding region, the harm people have done

content.cn_text: 即以所谓发展之名破坏森林。
content.en_text: by destroying the forests, all supposedly in the name of development.

content.cn_text: 动物学家认为，未来的突破在于人们如何根据这样的信息采取行动。
content.en_text: Zoologists believe the breakthrough that lies ahead is how people act on such information as this.

content.cn_text: 他们希望我们会学会把理性和情感联系起来，并应用这些知识来帮助保护我们的星球。
content.en_text: They hope that we will learn to connect our minds to our hearts and apply this knowledge to help protect our planet.

content.cn_text: 他们明确希望，人们对人类短视行为后果有更深理解后，
content.en_text: They express a clear hope that a greater understanding of the consequences of human short-sightedness

content.cn_text: 会迫切想在为时已晚之前纠正我们还能纠正的事情。
content.en_text: will make people anxious to rectify what we can before it is too late.

content.cn_text: 第4部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of part four.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40

items.title: The invention of computers is as significant as the discovery of how to create and use 31. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问计算机的发明和发现如何创造并使用什么一样重要。原文说 make fire and use it，所以答案是 Fire。

items.title: Computers will be seen as being 32. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问人们会认为计算机是什么样的。原文说 perceive these computer systems as intelligent，所以答案是 Intelligent。

items.title: Research will become much more 33. __________, and also more practical since more 34. __________ will be working in the field.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问心理学研究会变得更加怎样。原文说 become increasingly varied，所以答案是 Varied。

items.title: Research will become much more 33. __________, and also more practical since more 34. __________ will be working in the field.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问因为更多什么人进入该领域，研究会更实际。原文说 increasing numbers of women become research scientists，所以答案是 Women。

items.title: Imaging devices will give information about the behaviour of the 35. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问成像设备会提供关于什么行为的信息。原文说 inserted into the brain and provide information about how it behaves，所以答案是 Brain。

items.title: With increased life expectancy, it is important to consider the 36. __________ of elderly people's lives.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问随着预期寿命增加，重要的是考虑老年人生活的什么。原文说 take quality of life into account，所以答案是 Quality。

items.title: Knowledge of a person's genome will indicate whether they are likely to be affected by a 37. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问一个人的基因组知识能显示其是否可能受什么影响。原文说 likelihood of suffering from any particular disease，所以答案是 Disease。

items.title: The approach to treatment will be on an increasingly 38. __________ basis.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问治疗方法会越来越基于什么方式。原文说 a more personal approach，所以答案是 Personal。

items.title: For example, it is possible to see the effects of the destruction of 39. __________ in East Africa.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问在东非可以看到什么被破坏造成的影响。原文说 destroying the forests，所以答案是 Forests。

items.title: Such increased awareness should enable us to do more to look after the 40. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问提高意识应帮助我们更多地照看什么。原文说 help protect our planet，所以答案是 Planet。

groups.source_collect:

• With increased life expectancy, it is important to consider the $36$ of elderly people's lives.
• Knowledge of a person's genome will indicate whether they are likely to be affected by a $37$
• The approach to treatment will be on an increasingly $38$ basis.
Zoology
• People can explore the world via satellite.
• For example, it is possible to see the effects of the destruction of $39$ in East Africa.
• Such increased awareness should enable us to do more to look after the $40$.
